items.title: India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement
items.whyInNews: The Union Minister of Commerce and Industry, Piyush Goyal, and New Zealand's Minister of Trade and Investment, Todd McClay, held an industry interaction in Agra on April 26, 2026, ahead of the signing of the India-New Zealand Free Trade Agreement (FTA).
items.summary: The India-New Zealand FTA aims to boost bilateral trade between the two countries. The agreement is expected to increase trade in goods and services, and promote investment. The FTA will also provide a framework for cooperation in areas such as customs, trade facilitation, and dispute settlement. The agreement is expected to be signed soon, with the exact date to be announced by the governments of India and New Zealand.
keyPoints: India and New Zealand are set to sign a Free Trade Agreement (FTA) to boost bilateral trade
The FTA will cover trade in goods and services, and promote investment
The agreement will provide a framework for cooperation in areas such as customs and trade facilitation
The FTA is expected to increase trade between India and New Zealand
importantPoints: Full form of FTA is Free Trade Agreement, which was first established in 1934 in the United States

Policy implication: The FTA is expected to increase India's exports to New Zealand and promote economic growth

items.title: India's Civil Nuclear Programme
